lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Know of the duke if his last purpose hold,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Or whether since he is advised by aught

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To change the course: he’s full of alteration

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And self-reproving: bring his constant pleasure. To a Gentleman, who goes out.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Our sister’s man is certainly miscarried.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: ’Tis to be doubted, madam.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Now, sweet lord,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: You know the goodness I intend upon you:

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Tell me—but truly—but then speak the truth,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Do you not love my sister?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: In honour’d love.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: But have you never found my brother’s way

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To the forfended place?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: That thought abuses you.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I am doubtful that you have been conjunct

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: And bosom’d with her, as far as we call hers.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: No, by mine honour, madam.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I never shall endure her: dear my lord,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Be not familiar with her.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Fear me not:

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: She and the duke her husband!

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: I had rather lose the battle than that sister

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Should loosen him and me.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Our very loving sister, well be-met.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Sir, this I hear; the king is come to his daughter,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: With others whom the rigor of our state

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Forced to cry out. Where I could not be honest,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: I never yet was valiant: for this business,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: It toucheth us, as France invades our land,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Not bolds the king, with others, whom, I fear,

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Most just and heavy causes make oppose.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Sir, you speak nobly.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Why is this reason’d?

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Combine together ’gainst the enemy;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: For these domestic and particular broils

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Are not the question here.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Let’s then determine

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: With the ancient of war on our proceedings.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: I shall attend you presently at your tent.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Sister, you’ll go with us?

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: ’Tis most convenient; pray you, go with us.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: O, ho, I know the riddle.—I will go.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: If e’er your grace had speech with man so poor,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Hear me one word.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: I’ll overtake you. Speak.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Before you fight the battle, ope this letter.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: If you have victory, let the trumpet sound

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: For him that brought it: wretched though I seem,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I can produce a champion that will prove

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: What is avouched there. If you miscarry,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Your business of the world hath so an end,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And machination ceases. Fortune love you.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Stay till I have read the letter.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I was forbid it.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: When time shall serve, let but the herald cry,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And I’ll appear again.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: Why, fare thee well: I will o’erlook thy paper.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The enemy’s in view; draw up your powers.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Here is the guess of their true strength and forces

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: By diligent discovery; but your haste

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Is now urged on you.

lines.speaker: ALBANY
lines.text: We will greet the time.

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: To both these sisters have I sworn my love;

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Each jealous of the other, as the stung

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Are of the adder. Which of them shall I take?

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Both? one? or neither? Neither can be enjoy’d,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: If both remain alive: to take the widow

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Exasperates, makes mad her sister Goneril;

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: And hardly shall I carry out my side,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Her husband being alive. Now then we’ll use

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: His countenance for the battle; which being done,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Let her who would be rid of him devise

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: His speedy taking off. As for the mercy

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Which he intends to Lear and to Cordelia,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: The battle done, and they within our power,

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Shall never see his pardon; for my state

lines.speaker: EDMUND
lines.text: Stands on me to defend, not to debate.

stage_directions.text: Enter, with drum and colours, Edmund, Regan, Gentlemen, and Soldiers.
Enter, with drum and colours, Albany, Goneril, and Soldiers.
As they are going out, enter Edgar disguised.
Exeunt all but Albany and Edgar.

glosses.definition: Albany's scruple — he's uneasy about this war
private quarrels — Albany distinguishes public from private cause
Edmund's cool calculation between Goneril and Regan
Regan about Goneril — the rivalry explicit
suspicious — Edmund's coolness about Goneril and Regan

annotations.title: Edmund's soliloquy — 'Both? one? or neither?'
annotations.body: Edmund's 5.1 soliloquy is the play's coldest calculation: 'To both these sisters have I sworn my love; / Each jealous of the other... Which of them shall I take? / Both? one? or neither?' He concludes he will 'carry on my side' regardless of outcome — let the sisters kill each other and see who's left. This is sociopathic calculation as dramatic soliloquy. What makes the speech theatrically alive is Edmund's evident enjoyment; he is not tormented, he is amused. Shakespeare gives his villain a Machiavellian pleasure that neither the sisters nor Cornwall possess — they rage, he calculates. That calculative pleasure is also what undoes him: his very coolness leaves him open to being moved at the end ('some good I mean to do, / Despite of mine own nature'), a moment of late conscience that would have been unavailable to a villain of pure rage.

annotations.title: Edmund between sisters — Shakespeare's coldest triangle
annotations.body: The first twenty lines of 5.1 stage an immense amount of dramatic information in a few gestures: Regan asks whether Edmund has slept with Goneril; Edmund denies it with a half-truth; Goneril arrives moments later with an aside ('I had rather lose the battle than that sister / Should loosen him and me'). The sexual triangle is fully alive. Edmund's coolness between them is the play's most economical study of how to manipulate two rivals simultaneously — tell each what she wants to hear, let them act on the assumption you prefer her, and watch them destroy each other. The scene also advances the military: the armies are about to engage. Shakespeare interleaves erotic plot and battlefield plot in a way that makes each feel consequent on the other.

trials.prompt: What tone does Edmund's 'Both? one? or neither?' soliloquy strike?
options: Cold calculation with evident enjoyment — Edmund's Machiavellian pleasure is what distinguishes him from the raging sisters and Cornwall
